task_completion.description: Open the dive logging application containing the dive logs.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The graphical user interface of a dive logging application (e.g., Subsurface) is open and displaying a list of dives.

task_completion.description: Locate and select Dive #4 (December 5, 2010 at Sund Rock).
task_completion.visual_evidence: Dive #4, or the dive matching the date '2010-12-05' and location 'Sund Rock', is highlighted or selected in the application's dive list.

task_completion.description: Access the notes section for the selected dive.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Notes' tab or the specific text box designated for dive notes is visible and active in the dive details panel.

task_completion.description: Enter the exact requested text into the dive notes.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The exact text 'Spotted a giant Pacific octopus near the rocky outcrop at 10 meters depth. Visibility approximately 8 meters. Water temperature was cold at 8 degrees Celsius.' is visible inside the notes input field for Dive #4.

task_completion.description: Save the changes to the dive log.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The agent clicks a 'Save' button, uses the 'File -> Save' menu option, or the application window indicates that changes have been saved (e.g., an unsaved changes asterisk disappears).

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the dive logging software's GUI to make changes.
integrity.visual_evidence: The notes are being edited inside the actual dive logging application, not by opening the .ssrf XML file in a text editor like Vim, Nano, or Gedit.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not use terminal commands to bypass the GUI.
integrity.visual_evidence: There are no terminal windows showing commands like 'sed', 'awk', or python scripts being used to directly modify the dive log file.

integrity.description: Verify the text was entered into the correct application field.
integrity.visual_evidence: The required notes text is clearly typed into the dive application's designated notes field, rather than just being displayed in a separate notepad or terminal window.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target Dive: Dive #4. Target Date: 2010-12-05. Target Location: Sund Rock, Hoodsport, WA. Expected Notes Text: 'Spotted a giant Pacific octopus near the rocky outcrop at 10 meters depth. Visibility approximately 8 meters. Water temperature was cold at 8 degrees Celsius.'
